title: Who Gets to Validate You?
reading:
The question underneath people pleasing is always the same: Who gets to tell me I'm enough?

Validation, in its biblical sense, is the recognition and affirmation of a person's worth, feelings, and purpose — based on God's approval rather than human opinion. From the beginning, God has been answering this question. Not waiting for us to earn it. Not grading our performance. Simply declaring what is already true.

1 Peter 2:9 is one of the most striking declarations in all of Scripture: you are chosen, royal, holy, and His own. Not because you arrived that way. Not because you maintained it. Because God called you. He is the one who brought you out of darkness and into His marvelous light — and in doing so, He settled the question of your worth.

Romans 5:8 takes it further. God didn't wait until you were worthy. Christ died for you while you were still a sinner. This is not the validation of a God who approves of good people. This is the validation of a God who loves broken ones.

The trap of people pleasing begins the moment we forget this. When we look to others for what only God can give, we are reaching for a counterfeit. People's approval changes with the weather. God's does not.
reflection: Where are you currently looking for validation — and how does that differ from what God has already declared over you?
abide: Lord, remind me of who I am in Your eyes. Help me stop reaching for what You have already given.
